betterquesting:10.desc:8:
Spatial Storage lets you store physical things in the world such as blocks or mobs, and place them in a storage disk to be either placed back down, stored away, or even destroyed.

Firstly you place spatial pylons as a 3 dimensional axis coming out of the spatial IO port.

You place a spatial storage cell, power the IO port with AE power (not RF), and give it a redstone signal to store the things within the space in the cell.

How much space you can fit is based on the number for the storage cell, 2 for 2 blocks in each direction.

To take stuff out you simply place the cell back into the IO port and give it another redstone signal.

As I said you can store anything that fits within the area, blocks, fluids, mobs, and even players such as yourself. I do ask to not grief people by placing them in a storage cell and refusing to let them out though.
betterquesting:10.name:8: Putting the World in a Computer
